title: Accountant (6 months Contract)
description:
Key Responsibilities:

1. General Ledger & Month-end Closing

  • Post and review journal entries in NetSuite.
  • Prepare monthly balance sheet reconciliations.
  • Support month-end closing for the organization.
  • Maintain fixed asset registers, process additions/disposals, and run depreciation.

2. Reporting and analysis

  • Prepare internal management reports and variance analysis
  • Contribute to rolling forecasts and financial projections.
  • Provide financial analysis to guide decision‑making

3. Grant & Fund accounting support

  • Track grant utilisation and restricted funds according to non-profit requirements.
  • Assist in preparing reports for government agencies, donors, and grantors.
  • Ensure adherence to relevant compliance standards for childcare services.

4.   Audit & Compliance

  • Prepare and deliver PBCs; respond to auditor queries; maintain documentation and audit trails.
  • Prepare GST F5 returns
  • Ensure adherence to applicable accounting standards and internal policies.

5.  Accounts Receivable and Accounts Payable Coordination

  • Verify payment/refund/e‑claims batches against documentation and controls.
  • Review AR/AP related general journals for accuracy and proper coding.
  • Liaise with Finance Executives on schedules to ensure completeness ahead of close milestones.


Qualifications & Experience:

Education:

  • Degree in Accounting/Finance
  • Professional qualifications (eg, ACCA/CPA) are an advantage.

Experience:      

  • 2–5 years accounting experience, preferably with exposure to non‑profit, social services or service-oriented organisations.   
  • Experience with NetSuite or similar.
  • ERP systems.

Skills:

  • Strong proficiency in MS Excel (pivot tables, lookups). - Strong analytical, organisational, and documentation skills.
  • High attention to detail and accuracy.
  • Ability to work collaboratively within a medium-sized finance team.

Attributes:

  • Able to work effectively both independently and as part of a team.
  • Strong sense of responsibility and ownership.
  • Demonstrate high integrity and ethical standards- Self-motivated, attentive to detail with good time management skills.

postedCompany.description:
New Life Community Services (New Life or NLCS) is a social service agency in Singapore. We are passionate about serving the community and mentoring the next generation. We currently operate eleven childcare centres, two youth hubs, three student care centres, and one active aging centre. We conduct character mentoring programmes for children and youths, provide financial assistance to marginalised families with schooling children, and provide casework management and counselling to nip issues of poverty and debts.


Over the years, we have reached out to thousands of individuals from different religions and races, including children, youths and families – with absolutely no strings attached.


New Life is:

i) A Registered Society           

ii) Established by and affiliated to Covenant Evangelical Free Church 

iii) A Full Member of the National Council of Social Service        

iv)  An institution of Public Character
postedCompany.name: New Life Community Services
